entries.question: What is Tallyo?
entries.answer: Tallyo is a straightforward invoicing and business-records workspace for UK small businesses and independent operators.

entries.question: Who is Tallyo for?
entries.answer: Tallyo is designed for UK sole traders, freelancers, consultants, tradespeople and small service businesses that create quotes and invoices.

entries.question: What features are available?
entries.answer: Tallyo supports invoices, quotes, credit notes, customers, saved items, branded PDFs, document email, payment status, Stripe-hosted card links, recurring invoices, opt-in overdue reminders, refunds, exports and account-protection controls.

entries.question: What is the difference between a quote and an invoice?
triggers: difference between a quote and invoice
entries.answer: A quote sets out proposed work and prices before the work is agreed. An invoice requests payment for agreed or completed work. Tallyo can convert an accepted quote into an invoice.

entries.question: What is a credit note?
triggers: when to use a credit note
entries.answer: A credit note records a reduction to an issued invoice. It keeps the original invoice and the adjustment visible instead of treating the original document as if it never existed.

entries.question: How do recurring invoices work?
entries.answer: You save an invoice schedule, choose when it runs and decide whether each generated invoice should be emailed automatically. You can review or change the schedule when the work changes.

entries.question: How do overdue reminders work?
entries.answer: Overdue reminders are opt-in for each invoice. Tallyo schedules follow-up only after a saved invoice becomes overdue and you have enabled reminders for that document.

entries.question: How do Stripe card payments work?
triggers: how do stripe card payments work
entries.answer: Tallyo opens a Stripe-hosted checkout for the amount requested. The customer enters card details with Stripe, and Tallyo updates the invoice after the payment provider confirms the result.

entries.question: Does Tallyo store card details?
entries.answer: No. Customers enter card details on Stripe-hosted checkout pages, and Tallyo does not store full card details.

entries.question: Can Tallyo process deposits?
entries.answer: Yes. For a saved invoice, the seller can request the full outstanding balance or set a deposit amount for a Stripe-hosted card checkout.

entries.question: How do refunds work?
entries.answer: A supported full or partial refund is requested from the paid invoice. Tallyo updates the balance and activity after Stripe confirms the outcome. Another payment needs a fresh checkout session.

entries.question: Can I install Tallyo?
entries.answer: Yes. You can install Tallyo from supported desktop and mobile browsers for quick access. The exact browser action may be called Install app or Add to Home Screen.

entries.question: Does Tallyo work offline?
entries.answer: The app shell can open offline, but an internet connection is required to sign in and access or update authenticated business records.

entries.question: How do I create an account?
triggers: how do i create an account
entries.answer: Choose Create account on the Tallyo website, enter your details in the Tallyo app and complete the email-confirmation step.

entries.question: How do I log in?
entries.answer: Choose Log in on the Tallyo website to open the app. Use your confirmed account and complete authenticator verification when you have enabled it.

entries.question: Where can I find pricing?
entries.answer: The Pricing page shows the current plan direction. Exact prices, limits and availability are still being finalised, and this website does not offer subscription checkout yet.

entries.question: How do I get support?
entries.answer: Start with the reviewed Help Centre guides and common questions. A public business support contact will be added only after it is approved.

entries.question: Where can I read about security?
triggers: where can i read about security
entries.answer: The Security page describes Tallyo's current account, data-access, payment and browser controls together with honest limitations.
